channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Auburn's Broome says there's no pain in his elbow and he will be ready to face Florida in Final Four
item.description: Auburn forward Johni Broome returned to practice for the Tigers at the Final Four and said he has no pain in his right elbow and “no limitations” ahead of the Tigers’ matchup with Florida.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vertuno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Braves are desperate for recovery as they enter their home opener as the majors' only winless team
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Braves' longest winless start since 2016 has left manager Brian Snitker desperate for a recovery he hopes begins with the team's home opener against Miami on Friday night. Atlanta blew a 5-0 lead and lost 6-5 on Shohei Ohtani's ninth-inning homer on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FIFA president Infantino: US to host 2031 Women’s World Cup; CONCACAF may join project
item.description: “We look forward to sharing more details and unveiling our full vision for the 2031 FIFA Women’s World Cup in the near future,” the U.S. Soccer Federation said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: White Sox fall to Twins, 6-1, to drop rubber match of 3-game series after lengthy rain delay on South Side
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Harrison Bader and Byron Buxton hit home runs to back Pablo López's solid start, and the Minnesota Twins beat the Chicago White Sox, 6-1, on Wednesday after rain delayed the start by nearly 3 1/2 hours.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cubs, Seiya Suzuki look to stay sizzling when they return to Wrigley Field
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Pull out the hand warmers. Seiya Suzuki is headed home to Wrigley Field at last, and he promises to be well prepared for whatever chilly elements he faces in an unpredictable Chicago spring.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Nelly Korda settles for a halve and Hyo Joo Kim rolls on in LPGA Match Play
item.description: NORTH LAS VEGAS, Nev. (AP) — Defending champion Nelly Korda became the latest example of anything goes in the T-Mobile Match Play when she settled for a halve Wednesday against Brittany Altomare, No. 828 in the world and still building her game after an 18-month break from having a baby.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: British tennis pro born with rare genetic disorder collapses mid-match in terrifying scene
item.description: Francesca Jones, a British tennis pro born with a rare genetic disorder, collapsed during a match in Colombia on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kyle Tucker continues home run streak, Cubs top A's for 1st back-to-back wins of 2025 season
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Just one more game, then the Cubs will finally get to play at Wrigley Field. And they're aiming to return home on a three-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: 2025 Augusta National Women's Amateur
item.description: The 2025 Augusta National Women's Amateur is underway at Champions Retreat Golf Club in Evans, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: John Hart, Fox 5

item.title: Padres pick up another win, making for single best start to season ever
item.description: The San Diego Padres have made history after beating the Cleveland Guardians 7-2 at Petco Park on Monday, giving them a five-game winning streak to start the season for the first time ever.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Champions Retreat Golf Club ready to host Augusta National Women's Amateur following Hurricane Helene
item.description: The first practice round of the Augusta National Women's Amateur and other Masters related activities return to Georgia after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: SF Giants listed among Forbes' most valuable MLB teams
item.description: (KRON) -- As the 2025 Major League Baseball season starts in March, Forbes released its annual list of the most valuable teams. The San Francisco Giants were listed as the fifth-most valuable team in MLB, according to Forbes' list released on March 26.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Denny Hamlin ends 10-year win drought at Martinsville Speedway
item.description: MARTINSVILLE, Va. (AP) — At the track he loves to dominate, Denny Hamlin was back on top with a new face atop his pit box.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah snaps 3-game skid with 5-2 win at Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club continues to fight for its slim playoff hopes. Alex Kerfoot had a goal and two assists for the Utah Hockey Club in a 5-2 victory against the Chicago Blackhawks at United Center on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Diamondbacks rally for 8 runs in the eighth to beat the Cubs 10-6, split 4-game series
item.description: Lourdes Gurriel Jr. hit a game-tying, two-run homer, Josh Naylor had the go-ahead RBI double and the Arizona Diamondbacks used an eight-run eighth inning to rally past the Chicago Cubs 10-6 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lauren Betts and UCLA reach first Final Four of women's NCAA Tournament, beating LSU 72-65
item.description: SPOKANE, Wash. (AP) — Lauren Betts had 17 points and seven rebounds despite spending the entire second quarter on the bench, and top overall seed UCLA reached its first Final Four of the women's NCAA Tournament with a 72-65 victory over LSU on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Min Woo Lee holds off Scheffler and Woodland to win Houston Open for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Min Woo Lee kept his calm amid tremendous charges by Scottie Scheffler and Gary Woodland, winning the Houston Open on Sunday for his first PGA Tour title with the best lag of his life that set up a final par for a one-shot victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RSL gets shut out again at Minnesota United, 2-0
item.description: ST. PAUL (ABC4 Sports) - For the second straight game, and third overall this MLS season, Real Salt Lake failed to score a goal in a 2-0 shutout at Minnesota United Saturday night. Tani Oluwaseyi recorded a brace to help Minnesota United hand Real Salt Lake its fourth loss of the season. Dayne St.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Duke and Florida move on to Final Four
item.description: Florida and Duke won their regions on Saturday, with fellow top seeds Houston and Auburn set to play on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 9th-inning homer lifts Rays over Rockies 3-2 in Steinbrenner Field opener
item.description: Rookie Kameron Misner led off the ninth inning with his first major league home run, giving Tampa Bay a 3-2 win over the Colorado Rockies on Friday as the Rays began their season of home games at Steinbrenner Field.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gianna Kneepkens enters transfer portal
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Gianna Kneepkens, the leading scorer on the Utah women's basketball team has announced she is entering the transfer portal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
